summary: Decompiled the remaining ftpikachu5.c slice for Pikachu/Pichu special-move logic, adding src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu/ftpikachu5.c and removing nearly 1 KB of matching assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpikachu.s. The PR also adjusted action-state table references from lbl_* to func_* symbols, corrected Pikachu attribute field types from s32 to f32 where the code uses floating-point loads/math, expanded Fighter bitfield and state-var aliases needed by the C translation, and added the new object to obj_files.mk. There were no review comments in the slice; the PR body only says it was ready for merge.

key_files.why: New 369-line C translation replacing the removed Pikachu assembly slice, including functions func_801275CC through func_80128304.
Removed 998 assembly lines and updated data-table function pointers from lbl_* labels to func_* symbols.
Updated Pichu action-state table references to the newly available func_* symbols shared with Pikachu.
Changed ftPikachuAttributes fields xB4 through xD0 from s32 to f32 where the decompiled code uses them as floats.
Replaced generic UnkFlagStruct access for x2070/x2071/x2072 with named bitfields such as x2071_b0_3 and x2072_b4, and added x2340_stateVar1_u32.
Adjusted efAsync_Spawn prototype and added concrete prototypes for func_802B1DEC, func_802B1DF8, func_802B1FC8, and func_802B1FE8.
Added ftpikachu5.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES so the new C object replaces the assembly.

what_changed: Added ftpikachu5.c with C implementations for Pikachu/Pichu-related functions at addresses func_801275CC, func_80127608, func_8012764C, func_8012765C, func_8012779C, func_801277AC, func_801278BC, func_80127924, func_8012798C, func_801279EC, func_80127A54, func_80127ACC, func_80127B4C, func_80127BAC, func_80127C14, func_80127C74, func_80127CDC, func_80127D60, func_80127DE4, func_80127EC0, func_80127FB0, func_80128000, func_80128050, func_8012808C, func_801280C8, func_801280E8, func_80128108, func_80128128, func_80128148, func_80128168, func_801281AC, func_801281CC, func_801281EC, func_80128214, func_8012823C, func_80128264, func_8012828C, func_801282B4, func_801282DC, and func_80128304.
Removed the corresponding assembly bodies from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpikachu.s, leaving the remaining assembly file to reference the C symbols.
Updated Pikachu and Pichu action-state data tables so callbacks now point to func_80127CDC, func_801280C8, func_801281EC, and related func_* symbols instead of local lbl_* labels.
Added ftpikachu5.c.o to obj_files.mk immediately after ftpikachu4.c.o and before the remaining ftpikachu.s object.
Changed ftPikachuAttributes members xB4, xB8, xBC, xC0, xC4, xC8, xCC, and xD0 from s32 to f32, matching their use in floating-point calculations and lfs/stfs-derived code.
Refined Fighter fields around x2070-x2073 into named byte and bitfield members, including x2071_b0_3, x2071_b5, x2071_b6, and x2072_b4.
Added a u32 alias x2340_stateVar1_u32 for state variable storage, used when the value is passed to external helper functions as an unsigned handle.
Updated existing users in fighter.c and ftpikachu2.c from old nested bit access, such as fighter->x2071.bits.b5 and fighter->x2072.bits.b4, to the new direct bitfield names.
Adjusted function declarations in include/functions.h for efAsync_Spawn and item/projectile-like helpers func_802B1DEC, func_802B1DF8, func_802B1FC8, and func_802B1FE8.
smart_moves: The C translation was paired with header/struct corrections rather than forcing awkward casts everywhere; this made float-heavy Pikachu attribute code read naturally and better reflect the assembly's lfs/floating-point behavior.

The PR preserved unresolved address-based names where no semantic names were evidenced, avoiding speculative naming while still converting data-table references from lbl_* to func_* so C symbols could be linked.

Adding x2340_stateVar1_u32 alongside existing signed and float aliases let the code express handle-like calls to func_802B1DEC, func_802B1FC8, func_802B1FE8, and func_802B1DF8 without disturbing other uses of the same union storage.
The new Fighter bitfield names made translated conditions such as fighter->x2071_b0_3 and fighter->x2072_b4 much clearer than generic UnkFlagStruct access and enabled cleanup in existing files.

The implementation uses small helper logic, including nested_sum_fabs and fabs_inline, to reproduce repeated absolute-value comparison patterns found in the assembly while keeping the high-level flow understandable.

Callback assignments such as x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage = &func_8012779C and x21BC_callback_Accessory4 = &func_801277AC were represented explicitly in C, making action-state side effects visible for future fighter work.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling fighter special-move files, expect to touch shared Fighter layout: matching a local character file can reveal global bitfield names and union aliases needed by other code.
If an attribute struct member is loaded and compared as a float in assembly, update the header type to f32 rather than masking the mismatch with casts in the decompiled file.

For action-state table callbacks, replacing lbl_* table entries with func_* labels is part of moving code out of assembly; remember to update sibling character tables if they reference the same shared functions.
State-var unions often need multiple typed aliases. This PR added x2340_stateVar1_u32 because the same storage acts like an unsigned handle in external helper calls.
Prototype fixes can be necessary for matching. This PR changed efAsync_Spawn's signature and declared several func_802B1xxx helpers so calls in the new C file compile with the right argument shapes.

Use explicit unused stack locals or temporary copies when necessary to preserve stack layout and ordering for matching, as seen with unused arrays and fighter_copy variables in several decompiled functions.
Build-list updates are part of decomp completion: adding the new C object before the remaining asm object lets only the translated functions move to C while retaining leftover assembly.
naming_conventions: Function names remain address-based func_8012XXXX because the slice did not provide semantic names.
Action-state table labels are converted from lbl_8012XXXX to func_8012XXXX when the corresponding code becomes a C function.
Fighter bitfields around x2071 and x2072 use direct offset-and-bit names such as x2071_b0_3, x2071_b5, x2071_b6, and x2072_b4.
Union aliases on state variables preserve the offset name and add type/role suffixes, e.g. x2340_stateVar1, x2340_stateVar1_u32, and x2340_f32.
Pikachu attributes retain offset-style names like xB4, xB8, xBC, xC0, xC4, xC8, xCC, and xD0 when no semantic names are evidenced.
Callback fields are accessed through fighter->cb with existing descriptive names such as x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage and x21BC_callback_Accessory4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a large contiguous assembly block from ftpikachu.s only after adding a C object for ftpikachu5.c and updating obj_files.mk.
Kept table data in assembly but changed function pointer entries to C-visible func_* symbols, preventing unresolved lbl_* references after deleting local assembly labels.
Matched floating-point behavior by retyping ftPikachuAttributes fields to f32 and using fabs_inline-style absolute value logic for comparison sequences.
Used explicit stack padding/unused locals such as s32 unused[4], s32 unused[8], Vec vec1/vec2/vec3, and unused2 arrays where the translated assembly required stack shape preservation.
Used direct 32-bit clearing of flag structs via *((u32*)(&fighter->x2210_ThrowFlags)) = 0, mirroring assembly that stored zero over the full flag word.
Split common vertical-position absolute-value logic into an inline helper nested_sum_fabs to help reproduce the original instruction ordering.
Added or corrected external prototypes so calls to efAsync_Spawn, func_802B1DEC, func_802B1DF8, func_802B1FC8, and func_802B1FE8 could be expressed in C with the observed argument lists.
searchable_terms: ftPikachuAttributes xB4 xB8 xBC xC0 xC4 xC8 xCC xD0
lbl to func action state table
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_801275CC through func_80128304 to semantic Pikachu/Pichu move names.
Search for subsequent edits to ftPikachuAttributes fields xB4 through xD0 to confirm or improve semantic field naming.
Look for later Fighter bitfield layout changes around x2070-x2073 to see whether x2071_b0_3 and x2072_b4 naming was retained.
Search for func_802B1DF8 and related func_802B1xxx helpers to identify the subsystem behind x2340_stateVar1_u32 handles.
Check later cleanup PRs for semantic names around x2210_ThrowFlags and Pikachu callback behavior.
